page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. It features 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, and runs on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is allocated dedicated CPU resources and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of memory per core.

A key feature of this instance is the inclusion of 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, providing high-speed local instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance.

Performance benchmarks show a mixed profile. While Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores fall into the average range, stress-ng single-core CPU performance is categorized as poor, and multi-core performance is weak. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is average, but large block read and write operations are weak. The instance performs in the average tier for tasks like software compilation, image processing, and Redis SET operations, but struggles with larger workloads, as seen in the poor prompt processing speeds for 7B LLM models.

The c5ad.2xlarge offers a qualitative balance of compute power and fast local storage, making it cost-efficient for workloads that require temporary, high-speed storage without the overhead of network-attached volumes. However, the lower memory-to-vCPU ratio and weak multi-core stress performance are notable tradeoffs. It is best suited for web serving, moderate database operations, software compilation, and lightweight text or image processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance includes 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance across Geekbench tests, software compilation, and Redis operations, though stress-ng CPU tests and large-block memory bandwidth show weaker results. The instance represents a qualitative balance of dedicated compute resources and fast local storage, making it suitable for workloads like static web serving, moderate database operations, and software development, while its lower memory-to-vCPU ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering average performance for web serving and database tasks.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 300 GB NVMe SSD storage on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for balanced workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Equipped with 300 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Delivers average performance in software compilation and Redis database operations
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for static web serving, moderate databases, and development workloads
